title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Algebra Drill
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: If 2x + 7 = 15, what is the value of x?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><text x='40' y='32' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='18'>2x + 7 = 15</text></svg>
questions.solution: Subtract 7 from both sides: 2x = 8. Divide by 2: x = 4.

questions.question_text: A balance scale shows 3 identical blocks equal 21 grams. What is the mass of one block?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><line x1='100' y1='10' x2='100' y2='60'/><line x1='40' y1='60' x2='160' y2='60'/><rect x='45' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='95' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><text x='130' y='75' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>21 g</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Balance with three equal blocks totaling 21 grams

questions.question_text: Evaluate the expression 5n - 3 when n = 6.
Solve for y: y + 12 = 20.
Mia is 4 years older than her brother. If her brother is b years old, which expression gives Mia's age?
What is the value of x if x/4 = 9?
A number pattern follows the rule 2n + 1. What is the 5th term?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='35' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='16'>3, 5, 7, 9, ?</text></svg>

questions.question_text: If 6k = 42, what is k?
Tom bought p pencils for 2 dollars each. Which expression shows the total cost?
questions.solution: Cost is price times quantity: 2 x p = 2p.

questions.question_text: What value of m makes the equation m - 9 = 14 true?
Solve: 5x - 8 = 22.
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><text x='45' y='32' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='18'>5x - 8 = 22</text></svg>

questions.question_text: The perimeter of a rectangle is 38 cm. If its length is 11 cm, what is its width?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='30' y='25' width='140' height='50'/><text x='90' y='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>11 cm</text><text x='175' y='55' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>w</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Rectangle of length 11 cm and unknown width w

questions.question_text: Anna thinks of a number. She triples it and then adds 6, getting 33. What was her number?
If a + b = 15 and a = 9, what is 2b?
Solve for x: 2(x + 5) = 24.
On a balance, one triangle plus 3 coins equals 5 coins. Each coin has mass 1 unit. What is the mass of the triangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='30,70 50,40 70,70'/><circle cx='90' cy='60' r='8'/><circle cx='110' cy='60' r='8'/><circle cx='130' cy='60' r='8'/><text x='150' y='65' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='18'>=</text><circle cx='175' cy='60' r='8'/><circle cx='195' cy='60' r='8'/><circle cx='215' cy='60' r='8'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Triangle plus three coins balanced with five coins

questions.question_text: A taxi charges 5 dollars plus 2 dollars per kilometre. How many kilometres can you travel for 21 dollars?
Find x: 7x + 4 = 3x + 24.
A bag has red and blue marbles totalling 40. If there are 3 times as many blue as red, how many red marbles are there?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='12'/><text x='60' y='45' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>r red</text><circle cx='130' cy='40' r='12'/><text x='150' y='45' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>3r blue</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Red circle labelled r and blue circle labelled 3r

questions.question_text: If 3(n - 2) = 18, what is n?
The sum of two consecutive whole numbers is 47. What is the larger number?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='15' width='60' height='30'/><rect x='110' y='15' width='60' height='30'/><text x='38' y='35' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>n</text><text x='120' y='35' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>n + 1</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two boxes labelled n and n+1

questions.question_text: A square has side length (x + 2) and perimeter 36. What is x?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='100' height='100'/><text x='50' y='15' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>x + 2</text></svg>

questions.question_text: Ben has twice as many stickers as Kay. Together they have 54. How many does Kay have?
If 2a + 3b = 23 and a = 4, what is b?
A pattern of squares uses 4 tiles for figure 1, 7 for figure 2, 10 for figure 3. How many tiles for figure 8?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='82' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='104' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='140' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='162' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='184' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='206' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='228' y='20' width='20' height='20'/></svg>

questions.question_text: A father is 3 times as old as his daughter. In 10 years he will be twice as old as her. How old is the daughter now?
Two apples and a banana cost 8 dollars. An apple and two bananas cost 7 dollars. What is the price of one banana?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='25' cy='40' r='10'/><circle cx='55' cy='40' r='10'/><ellipse cx='85' cy='40' rx='12' ry='6'/><text x='105' y='45' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>= 8</text><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='10'/><ellipse cx='180' cy='40' rx='12' ry='6'/><ellipse cx='210' cy='40' rx='12' ry='6'/><text x='225' y='45' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='14'>=7</text></svg>
questions.solution: 2a + b = 8, a + 2b = 7. Subtract: a - b = 1, so a = b+1. Then 2(b+1) + b = 8, 3b = 6, b = 2.

questions.question_text: The expression 4x - 2 equals 3x + 9. Find x.
Lila bought pens at 3 dollars each and notebooks at 5 dollars each. She bought 4 more pens than notebooks and spent 28 dollars in total. How many notebooks did she buy?
questions.solution: Let n = notebooks, pens = n+4. 3(n+4) + 5n = 28, so 8n + 12 = 28, 8n = 16, n = 2.
